subtitle: Business Transformation · Founder-Operator
hero_sub: 15+ years of business transformation and delivery experience, including founder-level ownership of an AI-native commerce platform built and operated end to end. I diagnose where a process breaks, prototype the AI-first redesign myself, and convert what works into a framework the rest of the organization can run on.

the_case.need: 8+ years business transformation, product strategy, or technical operations, ideally founder or founding/early hire
the_case.answer: Founder of OVRSTCH (AI-native commerce platform, sole operator, 2024–2026); early engagement lead at a stealth-mode AI startup building fine-tuned models; 15+ years total business transformation and delivery experience.
the_case.detail: OVRSTCH is the literal match for 'founder' — I designed, built, and operated an entire AI-native commerce platform alone, with no team to delegate to. The stealth AI startup engagement put me in early, before process existed, translating ambiguous research goals into a working delivery model. Both are direct, not adjacent, proof of founder/early-hire experience.

the_case.need: Proven success leading cross-functional initiatives that reimagine systems or workflows
the_case.answer: Eptura: drove Agile/workflow transformation across ~10 distributed teams spanning Marketing, UX, Engineering, and Data — 35%+ efficiency gain. Crocs: redesigned release governance workflow with SOX-compliant SOD controls and a new 'Release Radar' tracking framework.
the_case.detail: At Eptura, functions that had never worked from a shared cadence — Marketing, UX, Engineering, Data — adopted a unified Agile rhythm under my coaching, producing a 35%+ efficiency gain. At Crocs, I redesigned the release governance workflow itself, not just facilitated it, building SOX-compliant controls directly into the process.

the_case.need: Strong technical literacy with mainstream and cutting-edge AI tools/automation platforms
the_case.answer: Daily practitioner of Claude Code, ChatGPT/GPT-4, and Google Gemini since 2022. Embedded Jira AI and Microsoft Copilot directly into client delivery workflows at Crocs. GitHub Actions CI/CD automation across every recent project.
the_case.detail: This isn't secondhand familiarity — I've been hands-on with these tools since near their public launch (Stable Diffusion in 2022, ChatGPT in December 2022) and use Claude Code daily as a working tool, not an experiment.

the_case.need: Hands-on experience building/deploying workflow automations, agents, or custom skills
the_case.answer: OVRSTCH: architected an automated print-on-demand fulfillment pipeline (Printify API) and embedded AI into planning/operations. 6 production applications built and deployed via Claude Code in 2026. Stealth AI startup: owned the proof-of-concept-to-production pipeline for fine-tuned model delivery.
the_case.detail: At OVRSTCH, the fulfillment automation wasn't a proof of concept — it ran the actual business, print-on-demand orders processed automatically via the Printify API. The 6 production apps shipped this year prove I can build and deploy, not just direct people who do.

the_case.need: Translate innovation into scale — convert PoC learnings into scalable frameworks/playbooks
the_case.answer: Stealth AI startup: converted ambiguous research objectives into structured backlogs, measurable KPIs, and a repeatable delivery framework, improving pipeline throughput ~30%.
the_case.detail: The hardest part of AI transformation work is exactly this translation — turning 'we got a promising result once' into 'here's the process that reliably produces it.' That's what I built at the stealth AI startup: a repeatable framework the team could run without me re-deriving it each time.

the_case.need: Advise leadership — trusted advisor to CEO, Head of Product, senior leaders
the_case.answer: Understood.org: primary point of contact for the VP of Engineering, advised on PMO structure and SDLC management. Eptura: produced recurring executive dashboards and acted as escalation point for senior leadership.
the_case.detail: Executive trust has to be earned fast in consulting engagements — there's no ramp period. At Understood.org I became the VP of Engineering's primary point of contact within weeks of a distressed program handoff. At Eptura, my dashboards were the source senior leadership used to make portfolio decisions.

the_case.need: Bachelor's degree in technical/quantitative field (preferred, not disqualifying per posting)
the_case.answer: Not held — BFA coursework at NYIT, incomplete. Addressed directly and honestly; the founder-operator track record is offered as the substitute proof point, consistent with the posting's own philosophy of valuing potential over credentials.
the_case.detail: I don't have a completed technical degree, and I'm not going to pretend otherwise. What I have instead is a founder who actually built and shipped the thing — which is the exact standard Tilt's own posting says it values over a transcript.

the_stack.headline: The agentic AI toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools used daily, not evaluated in a pilot.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT/GPT-4, Google Gemini — daily-use AI-assisted delivery and development
nodes.pm_role: Daily practitioner since 2022. 6 production apps shipped via Claude Code in 2026. Built fine-tuned models on Gemini/GPT-4/Claude at a stealth-mode AI startup.

nodes.label: Workflow Automation
nodes.desc: GitHub Actions CI/CD, Printify API fulfillment automation, N8N-class automation platforms
nodes.pm_role: Architected and ran an automated fulfillment pipeline at OVRSTCH via the Printify API. No direct N8N use yet, but the same trigger/logic/API-chaining discipline, applied repeatedly.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Administration-level Jira fluency plus AI-assisted reporting via Jira AI and Microsoft Copilot
nodes.pm_role: Embedded Jira AI and Copilot directly into Crocs delivery workflows, reducing administrative overhead ~30%.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps & Cloud
nodes.desc: Azure/Kubernetes delivery coordination across enterprise SaaS environments
nodes.pm_role: Delivered on Azure/Kubernetes infrastructure at Eptura and Crocs — coordinating release readiness and cross-team dependencies.

nodes.label: Commerce & Integration Platforms
nodes.pm_role: Built and operated a full commerce platform at OVRSTCH end to end, including payment integration.

when_things_break.scenario: AI initiative stalls in proof-of-concept — can't cross into production or scale beyond one team
response: At the stealth AI startup, the research team couldn't articulate testable acceptance criteria, so engineering kept rebuilding
Ran structured requirements elicitation sessions to surface real, testable criteria
Translated outputs into sprint-ready stories with measurable KPIs
Built a delivery cadence that turned into a repeatable framework, not a one-time fix
Result: throughput improved ~30% within two sprints

when_things_break.scenario: A solo operator needs to build and run an entire AI-first operation with no team to delegate to
response: At OVRSTCH, I was the only person — no team to hand anything to
Architected the commerce platform and automated fulfillment via the Printify API
Embedded Claude and ChatGPT into daily planning and operational decisions
Diagnosed a production bottleneck quietly costing 25% of cycle time and re-sequenced the workflow
Result: there is no substitute for having actually run the whole system yourself

when_things_break.scenario: Cross-functional transformation stalls because teams don't trust the new process
response: At Eptura, Marketing, UX, Engineering, and Data had never worked from a shared cadence
Built the Scrum-of-Scrums layer as the trust-building mechanism, not just a meeting
Standardized readiness disciplines across every team
Gave leadership real-time visibility into where trust was breaking down
Result: sprint carryover dropped ~20% and delivery efficiency rose 35%+

how_i_run_a_room: Every initiative starts with a diagnosis of the actual pain point, not the assumed one — before any tool gets chosen
Proof-of-concepts get built fast, with clear graduation criteria for what 'ready to scale' looks like
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks named before they become issues
Executive updates always include: what changed, why it matters, and what happens next — before anyone asks
Frameworks and playbooks get written down as they're validated, not reconstructed after the fact
Retrospectives produce measurable process changes — not action lists that expire

points: Founded and operated an AI-first commerce platform solo, from concept to production
Architected automated fulfillment pipeline via the Printify API
Embedded Claude and ChatGPT into daily operational decision-making
Diagnosed and resolved a production bottleneck, cutting cycle time ~25%

track_record.role: Engagement Lead / Agentic AI Delivery Lead
points: Owned the proof-of-concept-to-production pipeline for fine-tuned model delivery
Converted ambiguous research objectives into a repeatable delivery framework
Improved data pipeline throughput ~30% through root cause analysis
Coordinated across Data Scientists, Engineers, and C-Suite stakeholders

track_record.role: Senior Delivery Consultant / Business Transformation Lead
points: Drove Agile/workflow transformation across ~10 distributed teams
Unified Marketing, UX, Engineering, and Data under one delivery cadence
Drove 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover
Produced executive dashboards used directly for portfolio decisions

track_record.role: Business Transformation Consultant / Release Governance Lead
points: Redesigned release governance workflow with SOX-compliant SOD controls
Built 'Release Radar,' a real-time release-readiness tracking framework
Embedded Jira AI and Copilot — ~30% administrative overhead reduction
Protected an on-time go-live with clean post-launch stabilization

before: AI initiatives stay stuck in isolated pilots with no shared playbook
Executives have no visibility into what's actually working across teams
Each team reinvents the wheel instead of reusing a proven framework
New ways of working get mandated rather than adopted
after: A working proof-of-concept becomes a company-wide framework within weeks
Executives get visibility into transformation progress before they have to ask
Teams adopt new AI-first ways of working because they trust the process
Frameworks are written down and reusable, not rebuilt each time

the_ramp.deliverable: Review current AI pilots and experimentation efforts across functions, sit in on two operating meetings to see where friction actually shows up, and read whatever playbooks or frameworks already exist.
Identify the first high-value pain point and begin building a working proof-of-concept using Claude/N8N-class tooling.

Ship the first working proof-of-concept, convert it into a first-draft scalable framework, establish working relationships with three cross-functional stakeholders, and deliver a first executive readout on transformation progress.
